title: Middleware/EAI Alternatives and Portera Pre-Integration (Draft Fragment)
author: Katherine Jones (Aberdeen Group)
abstract: Draft fragment from Katherine Jones to Tom Dwyer revising a section on middleware/EAI alternatives for the Aberdeen Oracle ROI white paper. Defines middleware, documents a Senior VP of Infrastructure's negative experience with EAI tooling, and analyzes Portera's pre-integrated bundle (Oracle Financials + PeopleSoft HR + Siebel CRM) as a 'buy pre-integrated' alternative.
importance_rationale: Internal editorial fragment of the larger Oracle ROI paper — modest on its own, but captures Aberdeen's working middleware definition and a documented customer rejection of EAI tooling.
relevance_rationale: Pre-integration as an alternative to EAI remains a live enterprise pattern (e.g. composable SaaS bundles); Portera-style integrator-of-record models persist.
prescience_rationale: Anticipated XML-standard-driven integration improvements; accurately predicted that integrator viability (not just integrated-vendor viability) becomes a new risk vector.
